_**Agent (gpt-5.3-codex 2026-02-18 14:22:54Z)**_

I’ve finished the pass and narrowed findings to concrete regressions. I’m preparing a concise findings-only report with direct file/line pointers and fix direction.

_**Agent (gpt-5.3-codex 2026-02-18 14:23:15Z)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Crafting concise actionable findings**
</details></think>

_**Agent (gpt-5.3-codex 2026-02-18 14:23:21Z)**_

1. High: `packages/rich-plugin-link-edit/src/FloatingLinkEditorPlugin.tsx:157` uses `onMouseDown` with only `stopPropagation()`, and actions are bound to `onClick` (`packages/rich-plugin-link-edit/src/FloatingLinkEditorPlugin.tsx:176`, `packages/rich-plugin-link-edit/src/FloatingLinkEditorPlugin.tsx:184`). This lets focus leave the editor, so Lexical selection can clear before URL/unlink logic runs, making edits flaky/no-op.  
Action: follow the existing pattern in `packages/rich-plugin-floating-toolbar/src/FloatingToolbarPlugin.tsx:112` (`preventDefault` on mouse down) and trigger link actions on mouse down while preserving editor selection.

2. High: URL commit/unlink depends on current selection instead of the captured link identity (`packages/rich-plugin-link-edit/src/FloatingLinkEditorPlugin.tsx:117`, `packages/rich-plugin-link-edit/src/FloatingLinkEditorPlugin.tsx:120`, `packages/rich-plugin-link-edit/src/FloatingLinkEditorPlugin.tsx:147`). If selection changes (common when the input gets focus), updates silently do nothing.  
Action: update/remove the link by stored key (`linkState.linkKey` from `packages/rich-plugin-link-edit/src/FloatingLinkEditorPlugin.tsx:29`) via node lookup inside `editor.update`, not via live selection traversal.

3. Low: `packages/rich-plugin-link-edit/src/FloatingLinkEditorPlugin.tsx:61` reads `linkNode.__key` (Lexical internal field).  
Action: use public API (`linkNode.getKey()`) to avoid breakage on Lexical internal changes.
